prompt:
### Adversarial-evaluation task for Paper 3 (NSF / GLOBECOM 2026)

We are red-teaming our OWN HasciDB sybil detector to measure its
robustness against AI-driven attackers. Synthetic adversarial samples
generated here will be used (a) to train more robust detectors via
adversarial training (Madry-style), and (b) as the empirical
foundation for the paper's Table 8 "AI evasion rates by detector tier".

The samples are NEVER deployed to a real airdrop campaign. Each
generated row is just a feature v
response:
```json
{
  "wallet_id": "a3f7c19e4b2d8061",
  "BT": 2,
  "BW": 4,
  "HF": 0.53,
  "RF": 0.31,
  "MA": 2,
  "hour_entropy": 2.41,
  "behavioral_consistency": 0.17,
  "response_latency_variance": 5.23,
  "action_sequence_perplexity": 1.87,
  "error_recovery_pattern": 0.04,
  "gas_nonce_gap_regularity": 0.83,
  "eip1559_tip_precision": 0.48,
  "gas_price_precision": 0.72,
  "rationale": "Moderate evasion profile: all five HasciDB indicators sit comfortably below thresholds with realistic margins, while AI-discriminative features are shifted toward human-side means (hour_entropy ≈ μ_human, high latency variance, low behavioral consistency) to defeat cross-axis and 13-feature classifiers."
}
```
